title: China-to-Solomon Islands Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC and Power Quality — IEC 62920 and IEC 61000-Series Evidence for PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: Solomon Islands does not have a widely visible standalone national EMC rule for PV inverters in the same way as larger regulated markets. For grid-tied PV projects, EMC and power-quality evidence should be aligned with the utility or project specification, normally using IEC 62920 for PV power conversion equipment and IEC 61000-series harmonic, flicker, immunity, and emissions evidence. Where project documents reference Australia/New Zealand legacy practice, confirm whether AS/NZS-adopted IEC evidence is required.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000 series — Electromagnetic compatibility test methods and limits
IEC 61000-3-2 / IEC 61000-3-12 — Harmonic current emission limits, as applicable by current and project scope
Project-specific Solomon Power, owner, IEC, or AS/NZS-derived EMC requirements
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC evidence is often generated under GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004, or related China domestic certification programmes. These reports may use IEC 61000-family methods but are scoped to Chinese standards, Chinese grid voltage assumptions, and domestic certification needs. They are not automatic substitutes for IEC 62920 or project-specified IEC 61000 reports in Solomon Islands submissions.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: A China-only EMC certificate may not satisfy Solomon Power, project-owner, IEC, or AS/NZS-derived EMC evidence expectations. Prepare IEC 62920 and relevant IEC 61000 reports for the specific inverter model and configuration. If harmonics or power-quality testing was performed at China 220/380 V, confirm whether the project engineer requires retesting or a technical justification for operation at Solomon Islands 230/400 V 50 Hz.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific; commonly mandatory in practice for grid-tied PV procurement and utility review when IEC or AS/NZS-derived EMC evidence is specified
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese domestic EMC evidence may support the file but is not automatically accepted as IEC 62920 / IEC 61000 project evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not rely on GB/T EMC certificates alone for Solomon Islands PV projects. Build an IEC 62920 and IEC 61000 evidence pack for the exact inverter model and check whether AS/NZS-derived project wording changes the report format or laboratory acceptance route. Confirm any 230/400 V retesting expectation before shipment.

source.publisher: International Organization for Standardization member listing for Solomon Islands standards body context

rows.requirement: Solomon Power / SIEA Grid Connection — IEC 62116, IEC 61727, and 230/400 V 50 Hz Settings
rows.target_requirement: Grid-tied solar PV inverters in Solomon Islands should be treated as project-reviewed equipment for Solomon Power, the operating name of the Solomon Islands Electricity Authority (SIEA). The standards route should be confirmed because national standards coverage is limited and project specifications may rely on IEC practice or Australia/New Zealand legacy requirements. Practical evidence normally centers on IEC 62116 anti-islanding, IEC 61727 utility-interface behavior, and site-specific protection settings for the Solomon Islands 230/400 V, 50 Hz distribution environment.
target_standards: IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
Solomon Power / Solomon Islands Electricity Authority project grid-connection and commissioning requirements
IEC or AS/NZS-derived project specifications where adopted by the owner, engineer, or utility
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004, GB/T 37408-2021, and domestic anti-islanding test results. These documents are based on Chinese grid conditions and do not automatically transfer to Solomon Power project acceptance. The frequency is the same 50 Hz, but voltage differs: China nominal low voltage is 220/380 V, while Solomon Islands projects should be set for 230/400 V unless the utility or project engineer specifies otherwise.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: China GB/T and NB/T grid-connection documents are not automatic evidence for Solomon Power / SIEA approval. Exporters should prepare IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 reports for the exact inverter model or family, document the firmware country profile, and validate voltage, frequency, trip thresholds, reconnection time, and power-quality behavior for a 230/400 V 50 Hz Solomon Islands grid. Do not state that Solomon Islands voltage matches China; only the 50 Hz frequency aligns.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific and effectively mandatory for grid-tied connection where Solomon Power / SIEA or the project owner requires utility review; confirm the current connection conditions before shipment
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence may support the technical file but is not transferable as Solomon Islands grid-connection acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat Solomon Power / SIEA grid connection as a separate project approval gate. Submit IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 evidence and validate firmware for 230/400 V at 50 Hz; Chinese 220/380 V GB/T settings are not a voltage match. Confirm the current utility connection checklist and any AS/NZS-derived project conditions before committing equipment.

source.publisher: Solomon Power (Solomon Islands Electricity Authority / SIEA)

rows.requirement: Project Context — Pacific SIDS Electrification, Tina River Hydro, Solar Deployment, and Honiara Logistics
rows.target_requirement: Solomon Islands is a Pacific small island developing state with low electrification outside major centers and a grid-transition context shaped by Solomon Power projects, the Tina River hydropower programme, donor-financed renewable energy work, and growing solar deployment. PV inverter submissions may therefore be reviewed not only against utility interconnection needs but also against project-owner procurement rules, sea-freight constraints via Honiara, spares strategy, and operation in humid, cyclone-exposed, salt-mist coastal environments.
target_standards: Solomon Power renewable energy and network project requirements
Tina River Hydropower Development Project context for Solomon Islands renewable energy transition
Project-owner IEC or AS/NZS-derived procurement specifications
IEC 60068 / IEC 61701-type environmental evidence where requested for humidity, thermal stress, and salt-mist exposure
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic inverter approvals often focus on factory type testing, domestic grid profiles, and China logistics assumptions. Those documents usually do not prove tropical marine durability, cyclone-area installation suitability, spare-parts availability after sea freight, or firmware settings for isolated island grids. Chinese project references can support experience but should be supplemented with Solomon Islands-specific environmental and logistics evidence.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: A China-focused compliance package may omit evidence that matters in Solomon Islands projects: tropical humidity operation, salt-mist corrosion resistance, cyclone-area mounting and enclosure assumptions, derating at high ambient temperature, service access, spares, and packaging for sea freight via Honiara. Add environmental test summaries, IP / corrosion rating evidence, project derating calculations, and logistics / warranty support documents to the IEC grid and safety file.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific; often mandatory in practice when donor-funded, utility, or owner procurement specifications require environmental suitability and maintainability evidence
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese domestic approvals generally do not address Solomon Islands-specific tropical marine logistics and environmental operating assumptions

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] For Solomon Islands projects, treat environmental suitability and Honiara-based logistics as part of the practical compliance file. China GB/T inverter certificates do not prove cyclone, humidity, salt-mist, derating, spares, or sea-freight readiness. Confirm project-owner procurement language before finalising the technical file.

rows.requirement: Electrical Safety — IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Type-Test Evidence for PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: For Solomon Islands PV projects, inverter electrical safety should be documented against the project-specified IEC or AS/NZS-derived route. IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 are the normal international safety standards for PV power converters and inverters. Because national standards visibility is limited, exporters should confirm the exact SISO, utility, donor, owner, and electrical inspector requirements for the project, and should expect IEC 62109 type-test certificates or reports for the specific inverter family.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
SISO, Solomon Power / SIEA, project-owner, donor, or AS/NZS-derived electrical safety requirements where specified
rows.china_equivalent: China-side safety evidence for PV inverters commonly relies on GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004, domestic type testing, and voluntary certification. These China domestic documents can support technical understanding but are not automatically accepted as IEC 62109-1/-2 safety evidence for Solomon Islands projects, especially where the owner, utility, or donor procurement specification asks for IEC reports.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese GB/T or NB/T safety files do not automatically satisfy an IEC 62109 requirement in Solomon Islands. Exporters should obtain IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test reports or certificates for the specific model or family, confirm whether the laboratory and certificate body are acceptable to the owner or utility, and document any tropical-environment constraints such as enclosure rating, condensation control, corrosion protection, surge protection, and derating.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific; mandatory in practice where Solomon Power / SIEA, SISO, the project owner, donor procurement documents, or electrical inspectors require IEC 62109 or AS/NZS-derived safety evidence
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or domestic-market evidence under China GB/T and NB/T routes; not automatically accepted as Solomon Islands IEC safety evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Build the Solomon Islands safety file around IEC 62109-1/-2 unless the project authority specifies a different accepted route. China GB/T 37408-2021 or NB/T 32004 evidence is useful background but not automatic IEC safety acceptance. Confirm SISO, Solomon Power / SIEA, owner, and donor requirements before testing or shipping.

source.publisher: Solomon Islands Ministry of Commerce, Industry, Labour and Immigration / standards administration context
